text.content:
Located in Game Settings > Configure Settings > Custom D&D 5e > Configure Workflows

Create event-driven workflows that execute actions in response to triggers. Workflows can be configured at the world level, per actor, or per item.

Each workflow consists of one or more triggers (the events that fire the workflow) and one or more actions (what happens when a trigger fires). Multiple triggers use OR logic: any matching trigger will execute all of the workflow's actions. Each trigger can optionally have conditions that must also be satisfied on the actor for the trigger to fire.

Workflow Scopes

  • World-Level: Apply to all actors of the selected types (Character, NPC, or Vehicle). Configured in the module settings.

  • Actor-Level: Apply only to a specific actor. Configured from the actor's Effects tab via the Configure Workflows button.

  • Item-Level: Apply only when that item's events fire. Configured from the item sheet via the Configure Workflows button. Item workflows have access to additional item-specific actions such as Destroy Item, Equip/Unequip Item, Update Item, and Reduce Quantity.

Triggers define when a workflow fires. Click Add Trigger to add a trigger to a workflow. You can add multiple triggers to a single workflow; any matching trigger will execute the workflow's actions. Each trigger can also have optional conditions that must be satisfied for it to fire.

Rolls

Trigger

Description

Attack Roll

Fires before the actor makes an attack roll, allowing the roll to be modified.

Attack Rolled

Fires after the actor makes an attack roll.

Ability Check Rolled

Fires after the actor makes an ability check.

Saving Throw Rolled

Fires after the actor makes a saving throw.

Skill Check Rolled

Fires after the actor makes a skill check.

Tool Check Rolled

Fires after the actor makes a tool check.

Initiative Rolled

Fires after the actor rolls initiative.

Concentration Save Rolled

Fires after the actor makes a concentration saving throw.

Death Save Rolled

Fires after the actor makes a death saving throw.

Damage Rolled

Fires after the actor rolls damage.

Hit Points

Trigger

Description

Reaches 0 HP

Fires when the actor's HP drops to zero.

Drops to Half HP or Lower

Fires when the actor's HP drops to half or lower.

Loses HP

Fires when the actor loses any amount of HP.

Gains HP

Fires when the actor gains any amount of HP.

Combat

Trigger

Description

Start of Combat

Fires when combat begins.

End of Combat

Fires when combat ends.

Start of Turn

Fires at the start of the actor's turn.

End of Turn

Fires at the end of the actor's turn.

Combat End and Reached 0 HP During Combat

Fires for actors who reached 0 HP at any point during the combat when the combat ends.

Rest

Trigger

Description

Short Rest

Fires when the actor completes a short rest.

Long Rest

Fires when the actor completes a long rest.

Conditions

Trigger

Description

Condition Applied

Fires when a condition or status effect is applied to the actor. Optionally filter by a specific condition.

Condition Removed

Fires when a condition or status effect is removed from the actor. Optionally filter by a specific condition.

Effects

Trigger

Description

Effect Enabled

Fires when an active effect is enabled on the actor. This includes creating a non-disabled effect or toggling an existing effect from disabled to enabled. Enter the effect name to match.

Effect Disabled

Fires when an active effect is disabled on the actor. This includes deleting a non-disabled effect or toggling an existing effect from enabled to disabled. Enter the effect name to match.

Equipment

Trigger

Description

Item Equipped

Fires when an item is equipped.

Item Unequipped

Fires when an item is unequipped.

Counters

Trigger

Description

Counter Value

Fires when a number, fraction, or pips counter's value matches the specified condition.

Increase Counter

Fires when a number, fraction, or pips counter's value increases.

Decrease Counter

Fires when a number, fraction, or pips counter's value decreases.

Check Counter

Fires when a checkbox counter is checked.

Uncheck Counter

Fires when a checkbox counter is unchecked.

Counter Success Value

Fires when a success/failure counter's success value matches the specified condition.

Counter Failure Value

Fires when a success/failure counter's failure value matches the specified condition.

Per-Trigger Conditions

Each trigger can have optional conditions that must also be satisfied on the actor for the trigger to fire. Click the ​ Edit button on a trigger row to open the conditions editor.

Conditions are state checks — they test the actor's current state rather than reacting to an event. Available condition types include:

  • HP: 0 HP, Half HP or Lower

  • Combat: Start of Combat, End of Combat, Start of Turn, End of Turn

  • Conditions: Condition Applied, Condition Removed

  • Effects: Effect Enabled, Effect Disabled

  • Counters: Counter Value, Success Value, Failure Value

  • Custom: Custom Formula

All conditions on a trigger must be satisfied (AND logic). Multiple triggers on a workflow still use OR logic between them — any trigger whose event matches and whose conditions are all met will fire the workflow.

Example: A workflow with trigger "Start of Turn" and conditions "Half HP or Lower" + "Condition Applied: Frightened" will fire at the start of the actor's turn only if the actor is at half HP or lower and has the Frightened condition.

Custom Formula Conditions

The Custom Formula condition lets you write a JavaScript expression that must return a truthy value for the condition to be satisfied. This is the only condition type with access to the target token and target actor, making it ideal for relational checks like elevation differences or distance.

The following variables are available in the formula:

  • sourceActor — The actor making the roll.

  • sourceToken — The actor's token document (if available).

  • targetActor — The first targeted actor (if a target is selected).

  • targetToken — The first targeted token document (if a target is selected).

  • item — The item being used (for item-level workflows or weapon attacks).

  • config — The roll configuration.

Examples:

  • sourceToken.elevation >= targetToken.elevation + 10 — Source is at least 10 ft higher than target.

  • sourceActor.system.attributes.hp.pct > 50 — Source has more than half HP.

  • targetActor?.system.attributes.hp.pct < 50 — Target is at half HP or lower.

Trigger Conditions

Many triggers support optional conditions that narrow when the trigger fires.

Result Conditions

Post-roll triggers (Attack Rolled, Ability Check Rolled, Saving Throw Rolled, Skill Check Rolled, Tool Check Rolled, Concentration Save Rolled, and Death Save Rolled) support result-based conditions. These let you filter by whether the roll was a success or failure, or by the margin.

Condition

Description

Any Result

No filtering. The trigger fires on any result (default).

Success

The trigger only fires on successful rolls.

Failure

The trigger only fires on failed rolls.

Success Within

Fires when the roll succeeds by no more than the specified margin. Useful for detecting close calls.

Failure Within

Fires when the roll fails by no more than the specified margin. Useful for detecting near misses.

Success By

Fires when the roll succeeds by at least the specified margin. Useful for detecting dominant successes.

Failure By

Fires when the roll fails by at least the specified margin. Useful for detecting catastrophic failures.

Value Conditions

Post-roll triggers (Attack Rolled, Ability Check Rolled, Saving Throw Rolled, Skill Check Rolled, Tool Check Rolled, Concentration Save Rolled, Death Save Rolled, and Damage Rolled) and counter-based triggers (Counter Value, Counter Success Value, Counter Failure Value) support value conditions. When set, the trigger only fires if the die result or counter value matches the condition.

Operator

Description

Equal To (=)

The value must equal the specified number.

Less Than (<)

The value must be less than the specified number.

Greater Than (>)

The value must be greater than the specified number.

Not Equal To (≠)

The value must not equal the specified number.

Roll Subtype

Some roll triggers let you narrow to a specific subtype:

  • Ability Check / Saving Throw: Select a specific ability (Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma).

  • Skill Check: Select a specific skill (Acrobatics, Arcana, Athletics, etc.).

  • Tool Check: Select a specific tool proficiency.

If no subtype is selected, the trigger fires for any roll of that type.

Counter Key

Counter triggers require selecting which counter to watch. The dropdown only shows counters that match the trigger type: checkbox triggers show checkbox counters, numeric triggers show number/fraction/pips counters, and success/failure triggers show success/failure counters.

Condition Filter

The Condition Applied and Condition Removed triggers let you select a specific condition to watch for (e.g., only fire when Poisoned is applied). If no condition is selected, the trigger fires for any condition.

Effect Name

The Effect Enabled and Effect Disabled triggers let you enter the name of a specific active effect to match (e.g., Rage, Bless, or a custom homebrew effect). Matching is case-insensitive. If no name is entered, the trigger fires for any effect.

Actions define what happens when a workflow is triggered. Click Add Action to add an action to a workflow. Multiple actions can be added and will all execute when the workflow fires.

Counter Actions

Action

Description

Increase Counter

Increase a counter's value by the specified amount.

Decrease Counter

Decrease a counter's value by the specified amount.

Set Counter

Set a counter to the specified value.

Check Counter

Check a checkbox counter.

Uncheck Counter

Uncheck a checkbox counter.

Toggle Counter

Toggle a checkbox counter. If checked, uncheck it; if unchecked, check it.

Counter actions show a dropdown to select the target counter. The list is filtered to show only counters that are compatible with the action (e.g., Check/Uncheck/Toggle only show checkbox counters).

Condition Actions

Action

Description

Apply Condition

Apply a condition or status effect to the actor.

Remove Condition

Remove a condition or status effect from the actor.

Toggle Condition

Toggle a condition on the actor. If active, remove it; if inactive, apply it.

General Actions

Action

Description

Macro

Execute a macro. Drag and drop a macro from the sidebar or a compendium onto the drop area. The macro receives context including the actor, token, event details, die total, rolls, and additional data.

Update Actor

Update an actor property. Enter the property path (e.g., system.attributes.inspiration) and the new value. Multiple Update Actor actions in the same workflow are batched into a single update for efficiency.

Distribute Award

Open the D&D 5e Distribute Award form for distributing currency and XP.

Play Sound

Play a sound file. Use the file picker to select an audio file, or drag and drop a PlaylistSound from a playlist. Adjust the volume with the slider (0 to 1).

Request Roll

Request a roll from the actor. Select the roll type (saving throw, ability check, or skill check) from the dropdown. Optionally set a DC; if set, the result will show as a success or failure. Click the ​ Edit button to configure On Success and On Failure sub-actions (see below).

Roll Table

Draw from a rollable table. Drag and drop a table from the Tables sidebar or a compendium onto the drop area.

Update Token

Update a token property. Enter the property path (e.g., elevation) and the new value. Multiple Update Token actions are batched into a single update.

Workflow Actions

Action

Description

Enable Workflow

Enable a target workflow. Select which workflow to enable from the dropdown.

Disable Workflow

Disable a target workflow so it no longer fires. Select which workflow to disable from the dropdown.

Toggle Workflow

Toggle a workflow between enabled and disabled. If the target workflow is enabled, it becomes disabled, and vice versa.

Workflow actions let one workflow control another. This is useful for conditional automation such as enabling per-turn effects only while a toggle counter (e.g., Raging) is active.

Roll Actions

Action

Description

Add Roll Bonus

Add a formula such as 2, -2, or 1d4 to an attack roll as a situational bonus. The formula appears pre-filled in the dialog's Situational Bonus input where the player can review or edit it before rolling. Multiple Add Roll Bonus actions are concatenated with +.

Item Actions

These actions are only available in item-level workflows.

Action

Description

Destroy Item

Delete the item from the actor entirely.

Equip Item

Equip the item on the actor.

Unequip Item

Unequip the item from the actor.

Update Item

Update an item property. Enter the property path (e.g., system.quantity) and the new value. Multiple Update Item actions are batched into a single update.

Reduce Quantity

Reduce the item's quantity by 1.

Request Roll Sub-Actions

When using the Request Roll action, you can configure sub-actions that execute based on the roll's outcome. Click the ​ Edit button on a Request Roll action to open the result editor.

  • On Success: Actions that execute when the roll meets or exceeds the DC.

  • On Failure: Actions that execute when the roll falls below the DC.

Sub-actions support all action types except Request Roll (sub-actions cannot be nested). This is ideal for branching outcomes, such as removing a condition on a successful save or applying a new condition on failure.

Macro Context

When a macro is executed by a workflow, it receives the following variables:

  • actor— The actor that triggered the workflow.

  • token— The actor's token (if available).

  • event— The trigger event type (e.g., "attackRolled").

  • userId— ID of the user who triggered the event.

  • dieTotal— The die result total (for post-roll triggers).

  • rolls— The array of Roll objects (for post-roll triggers).

  • counterKey— The counter key (for counter triggers).

  • counterName— The counter's display name (for counter triggers).

  • counterValue— The counter's current value (for counter triggers).

  • data— Additional context data from the triggering hook.

Macros executed by the Attack Roll (pre-roll) trigger receive an extended set of variables instead:

  • actor, item— The source actor and item.

  • sourceActor, sourceToken— The actor and token making the attack.

  • targetActor, targetToken— The first targeted actor and token (if any).

  • config — The roll configuration.

Common Update Paths

Some commonly used property paths for Update Actor and Update Token actions:

Path

Description

system.attributes.hp.value

Current hit points.

system.attributes.inspiration

Inspiration (true/false).

system.attributes.death.success

Death save successes.

system.attributes.death.failure

Death save failures.

system.currency.gp

Gold pieces.

elevation

Token elevation (Update Token).

light.bright

Token bright light radius (Update Token).

Formula Values

The Update Actor, Update Token, and Update Item actions support formula values that reference actor data using the @ syntax. This lets you set values based on the actor's current properties or perform calculations.

Example Value

Description

@currency.gp + 100

Add 100 to the actor's current gold.

@abilities.str.value / 2

Half the actor's Strength score (rounded down).

@details.level * 10

Ten times the actor's level.

@attributes.hp.max

The actor's maximum hit points.

Formula results are always rounded down to the nearest whole number.

In addition to world-level workflows, workflows can be configured on individual actors and items.

Per-Actor Workflows

  1. Open an actor sheet.

  2. Navigate to the Effects tab.

  3. Click the Configure Workflows button at the top of the tab.

Per-actor workflows use the same trigger and action system as world workflows. They are stored on the actor and only fire for that specific actor. This is useful for character-specific mechanics like a barbarian's rage effects or a warlock's pact features.

Per-Item Workflows

  1. Open an item sheet.

  2. Click the Configure Workflows button.

Item workflows fire only when events related to that specific item occur. They have access to item-specific triggers (Attack Roll, Item Equipped, Item Unequipped, and Counter triggers) and item-specific actions (Destroy Item, Equip Item, Unequip Item, Update Item, and Reduce Quantity) in addition to the standard actions.

Item workflows are ideal for modelling item-specific behaviour such as cursed items, fragile weapons, consumables, or items that grant effects when equipped.

World vs. Entity Workflows

  • World workflows apply broadly to all actors of the selected types. Use these for table-wide house rules and universal effects.

  • Actor workflows apply to a single actor. Use these for character-specific mechanics or NPC-specific behaviour.

  • Item workflows apply to a single item. Use these for item-specific effects that should travel with the item.

All three types can be active simultaneously. When an event fires, matching workflows from all scopes are executed.

Below are practical workflow examples that a D&D group can use to automate game mechanics, add atmosphere, and create dramatic moments.

​

Note

Additional examples are available as separate drag-and-drop pages in this journal. Look for pages prefixed with Example: and drag them directly onto the Configure Workflows form to import them automatically.

Critical Hit Fanfare

Play a fanfare sound whenever any actor rolls a natural 20 on an attack.

  • Trigger: Attack Roll, Value: Equal To 20

  • Action: Play Sound — select your fanfare audio file

Fumble Effects

When any actor rolls a natural 1 on an attack, play a comedic sound and draw from a custom fumble table for entertaining mishaps.

  • Trigger: Attack Roll, Value: Equal To 1

  • Action 1: Play Sound — select a comedic fail sound

  • Action 2: Roll Table — drag and drop your fumble effects table

Battle Music Sting

Play a dramatic music sting when combat begins to set the mood.

  • Trigger: Start of Combat

  • Action: Play Sound — select a battle music sting

Victory Fanfare

Play a triumphant theme when combat ends to celebrate the party's success.

  • Trigger: End of Combat

  • Action: Play Sound — select a victory fanfare

Close Call Alert

Play a tense sound effect when an actor barely succeeds on a saving throw, succeeding by 2 or less. Great for building suspense.

  • Trigger: Saving Throw, Result: Success Within, Value: 2

  • Action: Play Sound — select a dramatic close-call sound

Dramatic NPC Death

When an NPC is reduced to 0 HP, draw from a dramatic death effects table and play a sound. Perfect for memorable villain defeats.

  • Actor Types: NPC only

  • Trigger: Reaches 0 HP

  • Action 1: Roll Table — drag and drop a Death Effects rollable table

  • Action 2: Play Sound — select a dramatic impact sound

Rage Mode

Use the Enable/Disable Effect triggers to activate a per-turn rage workflow whenever the Rage active effect is toggled. Set up three actor-level workflows on the barbarian actor:

Workflow 1 — Rage Start:

  • Trigger: Enable Effect > Rage

  • Action: Enable Workflow > Rage Rounds

Workflow 2 — Rage End:

  • Trigger: Disable Effect > Rage

  • Action: Disable Workflow > Rage Rounds

Workflow 3 — Rage Rounds (disabled by default):

  • Trigger: Start of Turn

  • Action: Play Sound — select a rage growl sound

Low Ammunition Alert

Get a warning sound when a custom ammunition counter runs low. First, create a number counter called "Ammunition" on the actor via Configure Counters, then set up this workflow.

  • Trigger: Counter Value, Less Than, 3, Counter: Ammunition

  • Action: Play Sound — select a warning alert sound

When a player character rolls a natural 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, award them Inspiration as a consolation prize.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "Consolation Inspiration",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["character"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "attackRolled", "operator": "eq", "value": "1" },
    "t2": { "event": "abilityCheckRolled", "operator": "eq", "value": "1" },
    "t3": { "event": "savingThrowRolled", "operator": "eq", "value": "1" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "actorUpdate", "updatePath": "system.attributes.inspiration", "updateValue": "true" }
  }
}

pages.name: Example: Cure Poison on Long Rest

text.content:
Automatically remove the Poisoned condition when an actor takes a long rest.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "Cure Poison on Long Rest",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["character", "npc"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "longRest" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "removeCondition", "conditionId": "poisoned" }
  }
}

An item workflow for a cursed amulet that inflicts Frightened when worn and removes the effect when removed.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import both workflows.

{
  "name": "Cursed Amulet - Equip",
  "entityType": "item",
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "equip" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "applyCondition", "conditionId": "frightened" }
  }
}
{
  "name": "Cursed Amulet - Unequip",
  "entityType": "item",
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "unequip" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "removeCondition", "conditionId": "frightened" }
  }
}

When an actor is reduced to 0 HP, apply the Unconscious condition.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "Death Knell",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["character", "npc"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "zeroHp" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "applyCondition", "conditionId": "unconscious" }
  }
}

A gritty realism house rule: apply Exhaustion when a character that was reduced to 0 HP during combat survives to the end of combat.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "Exhaustion on Combat KO",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["character"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "zeroHpCombatEnd" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "applyCondition", "conditionId": "exhaustion" }
  }
}

An item workflow for a weapon that degrades on a fumble. When the weapon rolls a natural 1 on an attack, reduce its quantity and run the Item Quality macro to track wear.

Configure this workflow directly on a weapon item via the item sheet's Configure Workflows button. Drag this page onto the item's workflow window to import.

{
  "name": "Fragile Weapon",
  "entityType": "item",
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "attackRolled", "operator": "eq", "value": "1" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "reduceQuantity" },
    "a2": { "type": "macro", "macroUuid": "Compendium.custom-dnd5e.custom-dnd5e-macros.Macro.SFV61B9hlon1uw0k" }
  }
}

Two pre-roll attack workflows that adjust attack rolls based on elevation:

  • High Ground: +2 bonus when the attacker's token is at least 10 feet higher in elevation than the target's token.

  • Low Ground: -2 penalty when the attacker's token is at least 10 feet lower in elevation than the target's token.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import both workflows.

{
  "name": "High Ground",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "triggers": {
    "t1": {
      "event": "attackRoll",
      "conditions": {
        "c1": {
          "event": "customFormula",
          "formula": "sourceToken && targetToken && sourceToken.elevation >= targetToken.elevation + 10"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": {
      "type": "addRollBonus",
      "formula": "2[HG]"
    }
  }
}
{
  "name": "Low Ground",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "triggers": {
    "t1": {
      "event": "attackRoll",
      "conditions": {
        "c1": {
          "event": "customFormula",
          "formula": "sourceToken && targetToken && targetToken.elevation >= sourceToken.elevation + 10"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": {
      "type": "addRollBonus",
      "formula": "-2[LG]"
    }
  }
}

At the start of each turn, a poisoned actor must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or remain poisoned. On success, the poison is cured. Uses a per-trigger condition to ensure the workflow only fires while the actor has the Poisoned condition.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "Lingering Poison Save",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["character", "npc"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": {
      "event": "startOfTurn",
      "conditions": {
        "c1": { "event": "conditionApplied", "conditionId": "poisoned" }
      }
    }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": {
      "type": "requestRoll",
      "roll": { "type": "save:con", "dc": "15" },
      "onSuccess": {
        "s1": { "type": "removeCondition", "conditionId": "poisoned" }
      }
    }
  }
}

Award gold pieces scaled by level whenever a character takes a long rest. A level 5 character receives 50 gold each long rest.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "Long Rest Stipend",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["character"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "longRest" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "actorUpdate", "updatePath": "system.currency.gp", "updateValue": "@currency.gp + @details.level * 10" }
  }
}

When an NPC drops to half HP or lower, request a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw as a morale check. On failure, the NPC becomes Frightened and a macro posts a chat message announcing their morale is broken.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "NPC Morale Check",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["npc"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "halfHp" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": {
      "type": "requestRoll",
      "roll": { "type": "save:wis", "dc": "10" },
      "onFailure": {
        "f1": { "type": "applyCondition", "conditionId": "frightened" },
        "f2": { "type": "macro", "macroUuid": "Compendium.custom-dnd5e.custom-dnd5e-macros.Macro.r999zcIKJy3CDhs9" }
      }
    }
  }
}

After a long rest, rolls a d20 and stores the result in a Portent counter on the actor for later reference.

​

Note

This workflow requires a number counter with the key portent (lowercase) and a max value of 20 on the actor. Create it via Configure Counters before importing this workflow. The counter key must match exactly or the macro will not update the correct counter.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import this workflow.

{
  "name": "Portent Roll",
  "entityType": "actor",
  "actorTypes": ["character"],
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "longRest" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "macro", "macroUuid": "Compendium.custom-dnd5e.custom-dnd5e-macros.Macro.pRt7VxYqLjMbKnWc" }
  }
}

An item workflow for a torch that emits light when equipped and extinguishes it when unequipped.

Drag this page onto the Configure Workflows form to import both workflows.

{
  "name": "Torch - Equip",
  "entityType": "item",
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "equip" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "tokenUpdate", "updatePath": "light.bright", "updateValue": "20" },
    "a2": { "type": "tokenUpdate", "updatePath": "light.dim", "updateValue": "40" },
    "a3": { "type": "tokenUpdate", "updatePath": "light.color", "updateValue": "#ff9329" }
  }
}
{
  "name": "Torch - Unequip",
  "entityType": "item",
  "triggers": {
    "t1": { "event": "unequip" }
  },
  "actions": {
    "a1": { "type": "tokenUpdate", "updatePath": "light.bright", "updateValue": "0" },
    "a2": { "type": "tokenUpdate", "updatePath": "light.dim", "updateValue": "0" }
  }
}
